findings.title: Critical Need for SSO/SAML Integration
findings.description: Enterprise prospects are unable to proceed with procurement due to the lack of SSO/SAML support. This integration is a non-negotiable requirement for security compliance, particularly for large organizations.
assumptions: Enterprise customers prioritize security features in SaaS solutions.

evidence.excerpt: Enterprise prospects require SSO via SAML 2.0 and OIDC before signing contracts.
David's team evaluated EnterpriseSuite for 6 months but procurement is blocked on SSO and data isolation.

recommendations: Prioritize the development of SSO/SAML integration.

findings.title: Need for Fine-Grained Role-Based Access Control
findings.description: Mid-market customers are struggling with the current permissions model, which lacks the granularity needed for effective project management. This results in inefficiencies and security concerns.
assumptions: Customers require customizable access controls to manage team permissions effectively.

evidence.excerpt: Enterprise customers need fine-grained RBAC beyond our current admin/member/viewer model.
Rachel described spending 3 hours per week manually adjusting access.

recommendations: Develop a more flexible RBAC system with custom roles and permission inheritance.

findings.title: Data Residency Compliance Requirements
findings.description: Enterprise customers, particularly in regulated industries, require strict data residency controls. Failure to provide these could disqualify the platform from consideration.
assumptions: Regulatory compliance is a key factor for enterprise customers when selecting SaaS solutions.

evidence.excerpt: GDPR and data sovereignty requirements mean enterprise customers must choose where their data is stored.
Kenji requires data residency in the Tokyo region for regulatory compliance.

recommendations: Implement region-pinned storage options and ensure compliance with local regulations.

findings.title: Need for Multi-Tenant Data Isolation
findings.description: Prospects are concerned about data isolation due to security audits. Proper isolation is critical for compliance and customer trust.
assumptions: Data isolation is a critical factor for enterprise customers to ensure security and compliance.

evidence.excerpt: Security audit flagged that tenant data isolation relies on application-level row filtering.
David stated that they can't deploy a SaaS tool that doesn't support SAML — it's a non-starter for our security team.

recommendations: Evaluate and implement proper isolation boundaries for multi-tenant architecture.

findings.title: Demand for White-Label Branding Features
findings.description: Several enterprise prospects are looking for white-label branding options to customize the platform for their own customers, indicating a need for flexibility in branding.
assumptions: Enterprise customers want to maintain their brand identity when using SaaS solutions.

evidence.excerpt: Three enterprise prospects require white-label branding with custom logos, color schemes, and domain names.

recommendations: Explore options for white-label theming and custom domain support.

summary: The analysis reveals critical insights into the needs of enterprise customers, particularly around security features like SSO/SAML integration and data isolation. There is also a strong demand for fine-grained role-based access control and compliance with data residency regulations. Additionally, prospects are interested in white-label branding options, indicating a desire for customization.
